title: Schedule Tracer Read Surface And Host Validation
objective: Implement the smallest bounded follow-on after the sandbox-complete schedule-drivers surface by adding a read-only schedule-tracer surface that explains predecessor or ancestor chains for a selected task through the existing GET-only bridge and PM review UI, without adding schedule writes, recursive mutation behavior, or client-side fabrication of dependency semantics.
dependency_note: UI-002f is no longer blocked by the 020f-era substrate findings. Packets 020e.1, 020g-a, 020e.2, and 020h have landed the truthful sandbox substrate, and UI-002e is now sandbox-complete on top of it. Delegate UI-002f against that landed sandbox substrate. If a true host-browser session is not available in this execution context, host validation should be deferred truthfully rather than fabricated.
constrained_outputs: One bounded GET-only tracer query or bridge extension returning root task, depth, parent-child chain records, and task labels for a selected schedule task
One minimal PM review surface for tracing why a task is constrained by its upstream chain

Focused test evidence proving the tracer surface works end to end from persisted schedule relationships without adding schedule-write behavior, plus host-browser validation evidence when a real host session is available or an explicit sandbox-complete host-deferred disposition when it is not
validation_steps: Confirm the tracer surface remains GET-only and read-only by construction
Confirm dependency traversal uses persisted schedule relationships rather than client-side invented graph data
Confirm no drag-to-reschedule, schedule writes, or dependency-mutation behavior is introduced

Confirm host-side browser validation succeeds for at least one representative traced task chain when a real host session is available; otherwise close truthfully as sandbox-complete with host validation deferred
sql_permission_note: Packet UI-002f authorizes only bounded GET-only bridge/query and read-only PM UI tracer work over existing persisted schedule data. No new SQL, no schedule-write behavior, and no client-side fabrication are authorized.
